commands.author: TurboTheTurtle

target.title: exec private-LAN access fails while same user GUI/LaunchAgent succeeds
target.body:
### Summary

Codex/OpenClaw `exec` cannot reach a private-LAN host from the Mac mini, while the same macOS user and host can reach it from GUI Terminal, Browser, and a one-shot `launchd` agent.

This presents as normal OS/network failures (`No route to host`, `curl: (7)`), which makes the operator debug the LAN even though the LAN path is healthy.

### Environment

- Host: macOS on Mac mini, OpenClaw gateway running as user `andy`
- Exec path: Codex app-server spawned by OpenClaw gateway
- Target host: `192.168.147.163` (`docker-arr`)
- Target services checked:
  - Lidarr: `http://192.168.147.163:8686/`
  - SSH: `docker-arr` / `192.168.147.163:22`

### Reproduction

From OpenClaw/Codex `exec` on the Mac mini:

```sh
curl -sS -I --connect-timeout 5 http://192.168.147.163:8686/
# curl: (7) Failed to connect to 192.168.147.163 port 8686 after ... Couldn't connect to server

ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 docker-arr hostname
# ssh: connect to host 192.168.147.163 port 22: No route to host
```

However, from GUI Terminal as the same user on the same Mac mini:

```sh
curl -I http://192.168.147.163:8686/
# HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
# Server: Kestrel

ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 docker-arr hostname
# docker-arr
```

A one-shot `LaunchAgent` bootstrapped into the GUI user context also succeeds:

```sh
curl -I http://192.168.147.163:8686/
# HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
# Server: Kestrel

ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 docker-arr hostname
# docker-arr
```

One confusing detail: `traceroute -n -m 2 -w 1 192.168.147.163` from `exec` received a one-hop reply from `192.168.147.163`, while TCP/SSH/curl still failed.

### Expected behavior

If `exec` is allowed to use host networking, it should match same-user GUI/LaunchAgent reachability.

If this traffic is intentionally blocked by an OpenClaw/Codex policy boundary, `exec` should return an explicit policy error instead of OS-looking network errors.

### Actual behavior

`exec` fails with network-looking errors while the same user and host can reach the target through GUI Terminal, Browser, and LaunchAgent contexts.

This led to unnecessary debugging of the switch/LAN/VPN path before discovering that the failure was specific to the OpenClaw/Codex `exec` process context.

### Related but distinct issues

- #76260 covers `exec` bypassing `web_fetch` SSRF blocking. This issue is the inverse failure mode: `exec` cannot reach a LAN target that the same user context can reach.
- #39604 covers adding an opt-in private-network setting for `web_fetch`.
- #38931 covers browser/private-network SSRF confirmation behavior.

This issue is specifically about the `exec` runtime mismatch and the misleading error surface.

### Security note

I am not arguing that private-LAN access should be enabled unconditionally. If the block is intended, the safer behavior would be a clear policy/approval error and consistent documentation, rather than a misleading `No route to host`.

target.title: doctor(config): auto-prune removed google-antigravity-auth entries
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: users can keep stale `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` config after the plugin was removed, which keeps emitting warnings (`#27371`).
- Why it matters: users must manually edit config after updates; startup/doctor warnings stay noisy until they do.
- What changed: doctor compatibility normalization now prunes stale removed plugin references from `plugins.entries`, `plugins.installs`, `plugins.allow`, `plugins.load.paths`, and `plugins.slots.memory`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider runtime/auth behavior changes; this only cleans stale config references.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #27371
- Related #13141

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw doctor --fix` now automatically removes stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin config references instead of requiring manual cleanup.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` present

### Steps

1. Add stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin references under `plugins.*`.
2. Run `openclaw doctor --fix`.
3. Re-check config and doctor output.

### Expected

- Stale removed plugin references are pruned automatically.

### Actual

- Confirmed via migration test coverage.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: ran `pnpm test src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts` and verified new migration test passes.
- Edge cases checked: removal covers entries/installs/allow/load path/memory slot in one pass.
- What you did **not** verify: full end-to-end doctor run on a live upgraded host.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unintended plugin config pruning outside removed plugin IDs.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: accidental over-pruning of plugin config if removal targeting expands incorrectly.
  - Mitigation: migration is hard-scoped to `google-antigravity-auth` and covered by targeted tests.

AI-assisted: yes.

target.title: feat(workspace): add tiered bootstrap loading with configurable bootstrapTier
target.body:
Closes #22438

## Motivation

Bootstrap files consume LLM tokens on every session. For users with large workspaces, loading all files into every session — including sub-agents and cron jobs — wastes context window budget on files the agent never references. This PR introduces tiered loading so users can control how much context each session type receives, **reducing unnecessary token usage** while preserving persona consistency where needed.

## What

Introduces a three-tier bootstrap file loading system (`minimal` | `standard` | `full`) with a new `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier` configuration option. Both the `minimal` and `standard` tiers exclude hook-loaded extras (e.g. `packages/*/AGENTS.md` from the `bootstrap-extra-files` hook); `full` keeps everything.

## Why

The current binary split (main=all, subagent/cron=minimal) doesn't support use cases where subagents need persona context or where main sessions should be lightweight. This adds a clean, backward-compatible middle ground that lets users optimize their token budget per session type.

## How

- **`resolveBootstrapTier(sessionKey, tierOverride)`** — resolves the effective tier from session type + optional config override.
- **`filterBootstrapFilesForSession(files, sessionKey, tierOverride)`** — extended with optional tier parameter (backward compatible — no tierOverride = existing session-type heuristic).
- **Hook-source guard** — both `minimal` and `standard` filters check `file.source !== "hook"` before allowlist matching, so a hook-loaded extra whose basename happens to be in the allowlist cannot slip in. This preserves the natural `minimal ⊂ standard ⊂ full` inclusion order on the real hook path.
- **Config** — `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier` in the zod schema, TypeScript types, labels, and help text.

The `source: "root" | "hook"` provenance field on `WorkspaceBootstrapFile` is the discriminator: root-level bootstrap files keep flowing through `minimal` and `standard`; per-package extras registered via the `bootstrap-extra-files` hook are only reachable through `full`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: tiered bootstrap loading — `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier` (`minimal` | `standard` | `full`) controls which workspace files are injected into each session's context window. Closes #22438. After the latest fix (`5cdaf8e774`), the legacy no-override default path also applies the `file.source !== "hook"` guard, so subagent and cron sessions stay root-only by default — matching the upstream session-type defaults this PR preserves.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2 on Apple Silicon, Node 24.13.0, pnpm 11.2.2, against the fork branch tip at commit `5cdaf8e774`. A reproduction harness at the repo root (`repro-tiered-bootstrap.mts`, not committed) imports the real `src/agents/workspace.ts` from this branch and invokes `resolveBootstrapTier` and `filterBootstrapFilesForSession` against an explicit `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` containing both root-loaded files and hook-loaded extras whose basenames collide with the root allowlist.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git checkout 5cdaf8e774` (this PR's head)
2. `pnpm install --prefer-offline --frozen-lockfile`
3. Author the reproduction harness at the repo root, importing `./src/agents/workspace.js` and calling the exported functions directly with a `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` shaped like a real workspace (root files + two hook-sourced `packages/*/AGENTS.md` entries).
4. `node ./node_modules/.bin/tsx repro-tiered-bootstrap.mts`

Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture, run live against commit `5cdaf8e774`:

```text

  main session      default=standard   override=minimal→minimal   override=standard→standard   override=full→full
  subagent session  default=minimal   override=minimal→minimal   override=standard→standard   override=full→full
  cron session      default=minimal   override=minimal→minimal   override=standard→standard   override=full→full

===== filterBootstrapFilesForSession — DEFAULTS (no tierOverride) =====

    - /tmp/proof-workspace/AGENTS.md  [root]

input  (1 root + 2 hook, basenames collide with allowlists)  →  3 file(s):

    - /tmp/proof-workspace/packages/core/AGENTS.md  [hook]
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/packages/persona/SOUL.md  [hook]

minimal  tier output  →  1 file(s):
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/AGENTS.md  [root]

standard tier output  →  1 file(s):
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/AGENTS.md  [root]

full     tier output  →  3 file(s):
    - /tmp/proof-workspace/AGENTS.md  [root]

===== BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY (no tierOverride passed) =====
  sess=agent:main:main                    → 10 files
  sess=agent:main:subagent:task42         → 2 files
  sess=agent:main:cron:nightly            → 5 files

Done.
```

Observed result after fix:
- Tier resolution: subagent and cron sessions default to `minimal`; main defaults to `standard`. Any session honors an explicit `tierOverride`.
- Legacy default no-override path: main = 10 files (all loaded including hook extras), subagent = **2 files** (legacy AGENTS.md + TOOLS.md root only — hook-sourced `packages/*/AGENTS.md` correctly excluded), cron = **5 files** (legacy AGENTS+TOOLS+SOUL+IDENTITY+USER root only — hook-sourced AGENTS.md correctly excluded). The hook-source guard introduced in `5cdaf8e774` closes the gap between `resolveBootstrapTier`'s reported tier and the actual filter output.
- Explicit `tier="minimal"`: 5 files (MINIMAL_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST root only). Hook-sourced files always excluded.
- Explicit `tier="standard"`: 8 files (STANDARD_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST root only). Hook-sourced files always excluded.
- Explicit `tier="full"`: 10 files (all loaded including hook extras).
- Regression guard scenario (1 root + 2 hook with allowlist-colliding basenames): minimal/standard return only the 1 root file; full returns all 3.
- Inclusion order across paths: legacy default ⊂ minimal ⊂ standard ⊂ full is a strict superset chain at every session type after the hook-source guard is applied uniformly across both the explicit-tier and no-override paths.

What was not tested: glob expansion of `loadExtraBootstrapFilesWithDiagnostics` against a live filesystem (the harness supplies the `WorkspaceBootstrapFile[]` directly to exercise the filter under study). No behavior with non-default agent roots was exercised — the harness uses the canonical session-key shapes `agent:main:{main,subagent,cron}`.


## Testing (supplemental)

`src/agents/workspace.tiered-bootstrap.test.ts` (14 it-blocks, all passing locally) covers the tier resolution and filtering behavior at the unit level, including the explicit `minimal ⊂ standard ⊂ full` inclusion guard added with the `2a3faf3151` fix. `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.test.ts` exercises the end-to-end hook path against real temp directories, including the updated assertion that hook-loaded `packages/persona/SOUL.md` does not slip into a subagent session.

## Changes

- `src/agents/workspace.ts` — `BootstrapTier` type, `MINIMAL_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST`, `STANDARD_BOOTSTRAP_ALLOWLIST`, `resolveBootstrapTier`, and `filterBootstrapFilesForSession`. Both `minimal` and `standard` filters apply the `file.source !== "hook"` guard.
- `src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts` — `source: "root"` provenance field on the root-load path.
- `src/agents/workspace.tiered-bootstrap.test.ts` — 14 it-blocks; new minimal-tier regression added alongside the existing standard-tier one.
- `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.ts` — re-applies the tier filter after extras are appended, honoring the configured `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier`.
- `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.test.ts` — refreshed subagent assertion to expect the corrected hook-exclusion behavior.
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` / `schema.labels.ts` / `types.agent-defaults.ts` / `zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts` — config plumbing for `agents.defaults.bootstrapTier`.

target.title: fix(subagents): reconcile timed-out waits against child session state
target.body:
Fixes #75785.

## Summary

Reconcile timed-out `agent.wait` results against the persisted child session state before announcing subagent completion.

This fixes a false-timeout path where the child run can finish successfully, but the parent-side completion handoff still reports `timed out` and falls back to a partial-progress stub.

## Problem / Motivation

The announce flow currently trusts the immediate `agent.wait` timeout result. In the observed failure mode, that timeout can be stale relative to the child session store state. When that happens, the parent announces a timeout even though the child session has already completed successfully.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts`
  - `waitForSubagentRunOutcome(runId, timeoutMs, sessionKey?)` now optionally re-checks session-store state after a timeout
  - added helpers to load and interpret the child session entry
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`
  - pass `childSessionKey` into `waitForSubagentRunOutcome(...)`
  - do the same for the immediate recheck path
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`
  - adds a regression test covering a timed-out wait that should reconcile to persisted child success before announcing

## Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail

The reconciliation only runs:
- when `agent.wait` returns `timeout`
- when the child session key is available
- for a short bounded retry loop

Normal successful and failed paths are unchanged.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Parent sessions stop receiving false `timed out` completion events for already-finished child runs in this path.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
child finishes successfully -> agent.wait returns timeout -> parent announces timeout -> actual child result is replaced by partial progress

After:
child finishes successfully -> agent.wait returns timeout -> announce flow rechecks child session state -> parent announces success -> child result is delivered
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8
- Runtime/container: npm global install
- Model/provider: openai-codex/gpt-5.4
- Integration/channel: subagent completion announce path
- Relevant config: default subagent announce flow with completion handoff enabled

### Steps

1. Spawn a subagent that performs many tool calls and completes successfully.
2. Let the child session reach a terminal successful state.
3. Have the parent-side announce flow observe `agent.wait` return `timeout`.
4. Compare the child session state with the parent completion event.

### Expected

- Parent completion announce resolves to child success and includes the child result.

### Actual

- Parent completion announce can resolve to timeout and replace the child result with a partial-progress stub.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios:
  - observed a child run with successful terminal state while the parent announce path still emitted a timeout result
  - applied equivalent reconciliation logic locally in the installed build and confirmed it corrected the announce outcome for that bug path
- Edge cases checked:
  - no reconciliation path is used unless `agent.wait` returns `timeout`
  - still falls back to original timeout result if the child session store is not terminal
- What I did not verify:
  - full upstream CI suite
  - multi-provider or multi-channel coverage beyond the local reproduction path

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: session-store terminal state could be briefly stale in the opposite direction
  - Mitigation: reconciliation runs only after a timeout and only upgrades the announce outcome when the child session entry is already terminal
- Risk: additional small read on timeout path
  - Mitigation: only occurs on timeout, with a short bounded retry loop

target.title: docs(skills/coding-agent): worktree-isolate long spawns + watchdog-trap mitigations
target.body:
## Summary

Adds operator-facing guidance to defuse a recurring failure mode in
multi-agent orchestration: a watchdog-reported "terminated" spawn
that actually kept running and silently raced the parent agent on a
shared working tree.

We hit this in real downstream work — the parent agent received a
harness \`produced no output for 180s and was terminated\` message,
treated the spawn slot as free, started doing per-file integration
work in the same project root, and then spent ~30 minutes chasing
phantom regressions before checking \`pgrep\` and discovering the
"killed" worker was still alive and overwriting staged changes.

This PR is the operator-side workaround. A complementary runtime
change to relabel the watchdog message itself (something like
"watchdog tripped — process may still be running; verify with \`ps -p
<PID>\`") would fix the root trap; the skill changes here are the
documented mitigations until that ships.

## Changes (all in \`skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md\`)

1. **Claude Code section** now distinguishes foreground \`--print\`
   (short tasks) from background long-running, where
   \`--output-format stream-json\` is preferred so the harness watchdog
   sees continuous liveness instead of silently ticking down on
   \`--print\`'s buffered output.

2. **New section: "Verifying a spawn is actually dead"** explains
   that the watchdog message is advisory, lists the working-tree
   symptoms of a rogue spawn racing the parent (files reverting,
   phantom untracked files, edits disappearing, unrelated test
   failures), and prescribes \`pgrep <agent-binary>\` as the first
   diagnostic step.

3. **Progress Updates section** gets one new bullet: don't treat a
   harness "terminated" message as a confirmed kill; verify before
   spawning a replacement. Points readers at the new section.

4. **Two new Rules entries** codify the above as hard rules:
   - **#12** — Worktree-isolate any long-running spawn that would
     otherwise share a working tree with the parent agent.
   - **#13** — Watchdog "terminated" messages are advisory, not
     authoritative. Verify with \`ps -p\` / \`pgrep\`. If \`--print\`-mode
     Claude Code spawns repeatedly trip the watchdog, switch to
     \`--output-format stream-json\`.

## Why these specifically

- \`--output-format stream-json\` for long backgrounded Claude Code is
  the prevention; \`pgrep + worktree isolation\` is the cure. Both belong
  in this skill because both are operator-facing CLI patterns.
- The new section sits between Progress Updates and the Rules block
  because it's a diagnostic playbook, not a rule.
- The Rules block already has 11 entries; #12 and #13 extend the
  existing \`~/Projects/openclaw/\` and \`~/.openclaw/\` "blast-radius"
  rules with the analogous "any project working tree the parent
  agent is using" rule.

## Test plan

- [ ] Read the diff (62 lines added, 1 deleted; one file).
- [ ] Confirm the new patterns don't conflict with existing skills
      anywhere else in the registry.
- [ ] If the runtime team wants to relabel the watchdog message at
      the source, this PR can land first as a workaround and that
      change can supersede the diagnostic section later.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(agents): canonicalize subagent list requester keys
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

  - Problem: `/subagents list` and related subagent control paths could miss runs when the requester was represented by a main-session alias such as `main` versus `agent:<agentId>:main`.
  - Why it matters: Users could spawn subagents successfully but fail to list or control them from the main session, making background work appear lost.
  - What changed: Canonicalized main-session requester/controller keys at the subagent list/ control boundaries and added regression coverage for both tool and slash-command paths.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to spawn semantics, permissions, session storage schema, thread binding, delivery behavior, or plugin APIs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #75593
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

  - Root cause: Main-session aliases were not canonicalized consistently before subagent requester/controller lookup, so equivalent requester keys could diverge.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests did not cover listing or routing subagents across equivalent main-session aliases.
  - Contributing context (if known): Subagent runs are tracked by requester/controller keys, and the main session can be addressed through more than one valid key shape.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file:
    - `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.scope.test.ts`
    - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-subagents-routing.test.ts`
  - Scenario the test should lock in: A subagent spawned under a canonical main-session key remains visible/control-addressable when listed from an equivalent main-session alias.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug was in requester/controller key normalization, so focused tool and command tests cover the affected lookup boundaries without broad E2E setup.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None for the alias mismatch case.
  - If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  `/subagents list` and related subagent control/list behavior now correctly find subagent runs from equivalent main-session aliases.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
  Before:
  [main session alias] -> [raw requester key lookup] -> [no matching subagent runs]

  After:
  [main session alias] -> [canonical requester key] -> [matching subagent runs listed]
```

## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No) No
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) No
  - New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) No
  - Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

  - OS: macOS local workstation
  - Runtime/container: Local Node/pnpm workspace
  - Model/provider: N/A
  - Integration/channel (if any): Subagents tool and slash-command routing
  - Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

  1. Spawn or register a subagent run associated with a canonical main-session requester key.
  2. Attempt to list/control subagents from an equivalent main-session alias.
  3. Compare behavior before and after canonicalizing requester/controller keys.

### Expected

  - Equivalent main-session aliases resolve to the same requester/controller scope, and the subagent run is listed.

### Actual

  - Before this fix, alias mismatch could return no runs.
  - After this fix, the run is found consistently.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
  Targeted verification passed:

  - pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.scope.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands- subagents-routing.test.ts src/agents/subagent-control.test.ts src/agents/subagent-list.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-subagents-spawn- action.test.ts
  - pnpm build
  - pnpm check:changed
  - codex review --base origin/main

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - Verified scenarios:
      - Main-session alias lookup fails before the fix and succeeds after the fix.
      - Tool-path subagent listing resolves equivalent main-session aliases.
      - Slash-command subagent routing resolves equivalent main-session aliases.
  - Edge cases checked:
      - Existing subagent control/list tests still pass.
      - Existing spawn-action tests still pass.
  - What you did not verify:
      - Live channel delivery in an external messaging integration.
      - Remote CI/Testbox execution.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

  - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

  - Risk: Canonicalizing aliases could unintentionally affect subagent lookup scope.
      - Mitigation: The change is limited to main-session alias normalization at requester/ controller resolution boundaries and is covered by focused regression tests for both tool and command paths.

target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.


## Live exact-head capture (2026-06-12): found and fixed an emit-side gap

Running the requested live scenario on an isolated sandbox gateway (loopback :28795, fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, Control UI webchat, native `openai-completions` lane with a tool-calling model) surfaced a real defect in this PR's original head: **the paused state never persisted in live runs, on any lane.** A store probe polling every 0.3s across four real `sessions_spawn` + `sessions_yield` turns at head `be96a422ff` captured the persisted row going `running -> killed` with `abortedLastRun: true` at yield time -- the exact pre-PR failure this PR exists to prevent.

**Root cause (probe-instrumented):** `sessions_yield` aborts the attempt mid-stream (`runAbortController.abort("sessions_yield")`), and the terminal lifecycle event can be emitted from the abort teardown before `run.ts` post-processing writes the `yielded` meta. The bundle-level instrumentation captured the failing ordering directly:

```
emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = undefined  aborted = false   <- event emitted first
meta.yielded set -> false                                          <- run.ts wrote 551ms later
```

The event reaches `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` carrying `aborted` without `yielded`, and `resolveTerminalStatus` correctly resolves killed. The projection/recovery/UI logic this PR adds was never the problem; the emit side never fed it. The unit tests passed because they fabricate events that already carry `yielded: true`.

**Fix (in `a46ba508b8`):** `onYield` now records `yielded` through the subscription's terminal lifecycle meta channel at tool time, before the abort, using the same late-binding pattern the yield abort itself uses (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). A regression test pins the handler half: an end event with `state.yielded` set and `aborted` resolved from the `isTerminalAborted` fallback still carries `yielded` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`).

**Post-fix live proof, end to end (same sandbox, instrumented bundle):**

```
10:27:40.065  [PROBE] meta.yielded set -> true              (onYield records at tool time)
10:27:40.423  [PROBE] emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = true   (event carries it)
10:27:4x      store: status=paused pauseReason=sessions_yield abortedLastRun=false
~10:28        Sessions Control UI: main session "Paused", subagent "Live"
10:28:1x      gateway KILLED while paused -> store unchanged (paused, ab=false)
10:28:3x      gateway restarted -> recovery skips the paused session;
              no "[System] Your previous turn was interrupted" prompt in the chat
10:29:20      subagent (orphan-recovered post-restart) completes -> announce drains
              the queued continuation in order -> running, pauseReason cleared
10:30:48      continuation finishes -> done, abortedLastRun=false
```

Reproduced again on a second pass (store probe `paused/sessions_yield` at 15:54-15:56 with closure-identity probes confirming the onYield-time recording). This covers all three asks: visible Paused state, restart skip, and orderly continuation, captured live at the exact head plus the one-commit fix.

**Codex lane now covered too (was a sibling-lane gap, now fixed in this PR).** The Codex app-server harness lane emits its own terminal lifecycle event (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` `emitLifecycleTerminal`) and was dropping the yield meta: the `phase:"end"` event carried only `phase`/`aborted`, never `yielded`, so the shared projection (`resolveYielded`) persisted Codex-backed yields as done/killed. The fix forwards `{yielded, livenessState:"paused", stopReason:"end_turn"}` on that event when `result.yieldDetected`, exactly as the native runner does, gated on the flag the `sessions_yield` tool already sets via `onYield -> onYieldDetected`. The Codex lifecycle event flows through the same `onAgentEvent -> server-chat -> persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent -> resolveYielded` path as the native lane.

Proven live on the Codex lane: a real `openai/gpt-5.5` turn through the Codex app-server (ChatGPT OAuth) that calls `sessions_yield` now persists `status=paused`, `pauseReason=sessions_yield` (was `done` pre-fix), and the subagent then drains the queued continuation in order back to `done`. An integration test (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.yield-lifecycle.test.ts`) asserts the Codex lifecycle end event carries `yielded:true`/`livenessState:"paused"`/`stopReason:"end_turn"` on yield and omits them on a normal turn (verified red without the fix).

Codex protocol behavior checked against the sibling `../codex` source (HEAD `b8aea2340`): `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/schema/typescript/ServerRequest.ts:19` defines the `{ "method": "item/tool/call", id, params: DynamicToolCallParams }` app-server -> client request, which is how the app-server invokes the OpenClaw `sessions_yield` dynamic tool (setting `yieldDetected`); turn lifecycle requests (`turn/start`/`turn/steer`/`turn/interrupt`) are at `ClientRequest.ts:90`. This change adds no new protocol dependency -- it forwards an OpenClaw-side flag onto OpenClaw's own lifecycle event.

target.title: feat(ui): replace textarea with CodeMirror 6 JSON editor in raw config view
target.body:
## Summary

Replace the plain `<textarea>` for raw config editing with a proper CodeMirror 6 JSON5 editor component.

### Changes

**New file: `ui/src/ui/views/config-editor.ts`**
- CodeMirror 6 editor with JSON5 language mode (handles comments, unquoted keys, trailing commas)
- Syntax highlighting, fold gutter, line numbers, active line
- Real-time JSON5 linting with error markers (red dot in gutter)
- Format/prettify button (JSON5-aware via `JSON5.parse`)
- Read-only mode via `Compartment` (live toggle without recreating editor)
- Dark/light theme via `themeCompartment`
- Full dark theme: `#1f2937` background, `#e5e7eb` text
- `errorOffset()` handles both JSON5 ("at line:col") and JSON ("position N") error formats

**`ui/src/ui/views/config.ts`**
- Replace `<textarea>` with `<config-editor>` component
- Fix dark mode binding: `?dark` now correctly follows `system` mode via `prefersLightScheme()`

**`ui/src/ui/views/config.browser.test.ts`**
- Update test: `<textarea>` → `<config-editor>` + CustomEvent("change") instead of native input event

**`ui/package.json`** — new dependencies
- `codemirror-json5`, `json5`, `lezer-json5`
- `@codemirror/{autocomplete,commands,lang-json,language,lint,state,view}`

**`CHANGELOG.md`**
- One-liner entry under `### Changes`

### Motivation

OpenClaw raw config uses JSON5 format (comments, trailing commas). Plain `<textarea>` can't validate or highlight it. The new editor provides real-time linting, format button, and proper dark-mode system-theme following.

### Test plan

- [x] ESLint / TypeScript checks pass
- [x] Config editor renders in light and dark modes
- [x] System dark mode correctly triggers dark theme
- [x] JSON5 linting marks errors with red gutter dots
- [x] Format button prettifies valid JSON5 content
- [x] Read-only mode disables editing
- [x] Undo/redo (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y) works
- [x] Browser test updated and passes

Closes #56401

## Summary

target.title: fix(ui): improve Thai locale copy
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the new Thai Control UI locale still had high-visible English fallback copy and a few unnatural Thai labels around common controls, the agent detail surface, files, channels, cron panels, and the language picker.
- Why it matters: Thai users see these strings immediately after selecting Thai, and the docs/UI translation pipeline needs one shared terminology style.
- What changed: added Thai UI/docs glossary terms, updated Thai translation memory, regenerated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts`, added a Thai copy regression test, and fixed the i18n generator process decoder so formatted multibyte locale output does not get replacement characters when stdout chunks split UTF-8 bytes.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no locale registry changes, no supported-locale changes, no docs generated-output edits under `docs/th/**`, no Mintlify/navigation behavior changes, and no public API or runtime contract changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #70510
- Related #3460
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Thai was recently enabled in the Control UI picker, but the Thai translation memory still carried English fallbacks and weaker terminology for high-visible agent/file/channel labels. While regenerating, the formatter subprocess output path could also decode UTF-8 chunks with `String(chunk)`, which can corrupt Thai text if a multibyte character is split across chunks.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing i18n tests checked structural alignment and non-English fallback health, but did not lock specific Thai high-visible labels or replacement-character regressions.
- Contributing context (if known): #70510 tracks broader Thai docs feedback, so this PR intentionally updates glossary sources and Control UI copy without editing generated docs output.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Thai common, language picker, agent, file preview, and cron delivery copy stays localized and does not contain the prior replacement-character corruption.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these keys are generated output consumed directly by the Control UI, so a locale-level unit assertion catches both TM drift and generator corruption.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): structural locale alignment already existed in the same file, but it did not assert Thai wording quality.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Thai Control UI copy is more natural for high-visible labels such as Agent, Workspace, Tools, Channel, Default, Preview, language names, and file preview controls. Future Thai docs/UI translation runs also get the updated mixed technical glossary.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [mixed English fallbacks and awkward labels]

After:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [Thai localized labels using shared glossary terms]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4.1
- Runtime/container: Node v24.13.0, pnpm 10.33.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI i18n
- Relevant config (redacted): no translation provider configured; existing TM cache used

### Steps

1. Regenerate Thai Control UI locale from source translation memory.
2. Run i18n drift, targeted locale tests, docs glossary checks, formatter checks, and changed gate.
3. Inspect the generated Thai bundle for replacement-character corruption.

### Expected

- Thai locale bundle stays generator-clean, high-visible Thai labels are localized, and glossary source files stay valid.

### Actual

- Expected behavior confirmed locally.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording

- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because the local `blacksmith` CLI is not installed. The unpinned remote installer was not executed; the local changed gate above was used as the fallback.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: regenerated Thai locale from TM, verified `ui:i18n:check` clean for all locales, verified Thai high-visible copy assertions, verified docs Thai glossary source check, verified no replacement characters remain in the Thai locale/TM, and verified local changed gate.

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Thai wording preferences can be subjective.
  - Mitigation: kept the scope to high-visible UI labels and encoded the mixed technical style in both UI and docs glossaries so future translation passes stay consistent.
- Risk: generator script change touches shared tooling.
  - Mitigation: narrowed the change to UTF-8 stream decoding for subprocess output and covered the observed Thai replacement-character failure with a locale test plus `ui:i18n:check`.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Thai Control UI high-visible labels now load from the regenerated Thai locale bundle with localized agent, workspace, channel, file-preview, and language picker copy instead of the prior English/awkward fallback wording.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS 26.4.1 with Node v24.13.0 and pnpm 10.33.2, using the generated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts` bundle after the patch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Loaded the generated Thai Control UI locale bundle with Node/tsx and printed the labels a Thai user would see in the agent/file/language picker surfaces.

target.title: fix(agents): route sessions_send A2A announce replies back to request…
target.body:
Fixes inter-agent communication bug where sessions_send replies were not being delivered back to the requesting agent's session.

Problem: When agent A sends a message to agent B via sessions_send, agent B processes the message but the reply stays in agent B's own session instead of being delivered back to agent A. This causes sessions_send to timeout since the reply never reaches the requester.

Root Cause: The A2A announce flow was sending replies to the target agent's channel instead of back to the requester's session.

Solution: Modified the announce delivery logic to prioritize sending replies back to the requester's session via the agent method with deliver: true, while maintaining backward compatibility with a fallback to channel delivery when no requester session exists.

Impact:

Inter-agent communication now works correctly
Replies are delivered to the requesting agent's session
sessions_send no longer times out waiting for replies
Multi-agent coordination and cron job result reporting now function as expected
Fixes #76484

target.title: Fix/cron duplicate name validation
target.body:
Fixes #76160

\`openclaw cron add\` and \`cron edit\` now enforce unique job names, preventing silent duplicate-name acceptance that could lead to dual-fire risk when both jobs remain enabled.

## Problem

Previously, the cron service silently accepted jobs with duplicate names:
- Two \`cron add\` calls with the same name created two distinct rows with different IDs
- \`cron edit\` could rename a job onto an existing name
- Default \`cron list\` only showed one job when their \`enabled\` states differed, hiding the divergence
- If both jobs remained enabled, they would fire twice per cycle with potentially divergent prompts

## Solution

Added name uniqueness validation in the service layer:
- **\`cron.add\`**: Checks for existing job with the same name before creating
- **\`cron.update\`**: Checks for name collision when renaming (allows no-op self-rename)
- **Disabled jobs**: Included in uniqueness check to prevent the exact scenario from the bug report
- **Error messages**: Clear, actionable format with existing job ID and removal instructions

## Implementation

- \`src/cron/service/ops.ts\`: Added \`findJobByName()\` checks in \`add()\` and \`update()\`
- \`src/cron/service/jobs.ts\`: Added \`findJobByName()\` helper function
- \`src/cron/service.duplicate-names.test.ts\`: Comprehensive test suite (13 test cases)

All entry points (CLI, agent tool, gateway RPC) funnel through the service layer, so validation is enforced everywhere.

## Testing

Added dedicated test suite covering:
- Adding jobs with duplicate names (enabled and disabled)
- Renaming jobs to existing names (enabled and disabled)
- Edge cases (case sensitivity, special characters, empty names)
- No-op renames (renaming to same name)
- Error message validation

## Breaking Changes

None. This is a bug fix that prevents invalid state. Operators who were accidentally creating duplicate names will now receive clear error messages with instructions to resolve the conflict.

## Related

- Supersedes closed PR #76180
- Found during production config audit in markthebest12/openclaw-infra#1305" --base main

target.title: fix(diagnostics): rotate cache trace and payload JSONL logs
target.body:
## Summary
- Add opt-in archive rotation to the queued JSONL writer while preserving cap-and-drop behavior for existing maxFileBytes-only callers.
- Bound diagnostic JSONL sinks by default: cache trace rotates at 50 MB with 3 archives; Anthropic provider payload logs rotate at 100 MB with 5 archives.
- Add config/env wiring, generated schema metadata, docs, and changelog entries for the new caps and archive retention controls.

## Impact
This is an operational safety fix. In real local usage, cache-trace JSONL reached 46 GB after only a couple months of OpenClaw use. Because these diagnostic files had no rotation or cap, temporary forensic tracing could silently become a disk and memory pressure source, and large trace files can strain tools during restart/recovery enough to freeze or destabilize a setup.

## Testing
- pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/queued-file-writer.test.ts src/agents/cache-trace.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-payload-log.test.ts src/config/logging-max-file-bytes.test.ts src/infra/dotenv.test.ts

target.title: fix(install): trap SIGINT so Ctrl+C exits cleanly during upgrade doctor
target.body:
Fixes #90011
Fixes #82304

## Problem

When running `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, pressing Ctrl+C during `openclaw doctor` in the upgrade flow does not abort the installer. The SIGINT kills the doctor subprocess but the installer catches it as a regular failure, continues past the prompt, and opens a browser to an unconfigured dashboard URL.

## Fix

1. **Add SIGINT/SIGTERM trap**: `abort_install_int`/`abort_install_term` handlers clean up temp files, show a warning, and exit 130/143
2. **Propagate doctor exit code 130**: capture `openclaw doctor` exit code separately; if 130 (SIGINT), call `abort_install_int` instead of treating it as a regular failure
3. **Preserve signal exit codes through `run_quiet_step`**: the wrapper previously normalized all failures to exit 1, which swallowed SIGINT (130). Now returns signal exit codes (>128) so the doctor exit-130 check fires correctly, while preserving exit 1 for non-signal failures to maintain existing caller semantics across all 66+ installer call sites
4. **Clear dashboard flag on doctor failure**: when the interactive upgrade-doctor exits non-zero (Clack cancellation exit 1, SIGINT exit 130, or ordinary failure), clear `should_open_dashboard` so the installer does not launch a dead dashboard after an incomplete upgrade
5. **Guard every run_doctor caller**: the existing-config fresh-install path called `run_doctor` without checking its return value, so a failed doctor would still set `should_open_dashboard=true`. Now guarded with `if run_doctor; then should_open_dashboard=true; fi`
6. **Fix Clack cancel exit code**: `guardCancel` in `onboard-helpers.ts` called `runtime.exit(0)` when the user cancelled at a Clack prompt (Escape key), making the install script treat cancellation as success. Changed to `runtime.exit(1)` to match the `WizardCancelledError` pattern used by `clack-prompter.ts` and `onboard-interactive.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** (1) Pressing Ctrl+C during `openclaw doctor` in the upgrade flow does not abort the installer; the installer continues and opens a dead dashboard. (2) `guardCancel` in onboard-helpers exited with 0/1 instead of 130, so the installer could not distinguish user cancellation from regular failure.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v26.3.0, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/wt-76386`. Gateway started and doctor command exercised on a real OpenClaw deployment.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  Step 1. Built from branch: `CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`

  Step 2. Verified the fix in the compiled production bundle (before vs after).

  Step 3. Started the patched gateway and ran `openclaw doctor --non-interactive` to confirm the doctor module works correctly.

  Step 4. Sent SIGINT to the installer during a real upgrade to verify the abort path.

  Step 5. Ran the full install script test suite (49 tests).

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  **Before (upstream/main):** `guardCancel` exits with 0, making the installer treat Clack cancellation as success:

  ```console
  $ sed -n '27,31p' /tmp/oc-base/dist/onboard-helpers-DxyrQbNM.js
  function guardCancel(value, runtime) {
  if (isCancel(value)) {
  cancel(stylePromptTitle("Setup cancelled.") ?? "Setup cancelled.");
  runtime.exit(0);
  throw new Error("unreachable");
  ```

  **After (patched):** `guardCancel` exits with 130 (SIGINT convention), which the installer detects and aborts:

  ```console
  $ sed -n '27,31p' /tmp/wt-76386/dist/onboard-helpers-D47vq5rt.js
  function guardCancel(value, runtime) {
  if (isCancel(value)) {
  cancel(stylePromptTitle("Setup cancelled.") ?? "Setup cancelled.");
  runtime.exit(130);
  throw new Error("unreachable");
  ```

  **Install script `run_doctor` now detects exit 130:**

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'doctor_exit.*130\|abort_install_int' /tmp/wt-76386/scripts/install.sh
  35:abort_install_int() {
  47:trap abort_install_int INT
  2835:    if (( doctor_exit == 130 )); then
  2836:        abort_install_int
  3303:            if (( doctor_exit == 130 )); then
  ```

  **Doctor runs correctly on the live deployment:**

  ```console
  $ cd /tmp/wt-76386 && timeout 5 node openclaw.mjs doctor --non-interactive
  OPENCLAW
  OpenClaw doctor
  Doctor warnings
    - Left migrated plugin install index in place because archive already exists
  [state-migrations] Legacy state migration warnings:
    - Left migrated plugin install index in place
  $ echo "exit code: $?"
  exit code: 0
  ```

  **Gateway starts cleanly with the patched module:**

  ```console
  $ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
  [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins; 1.0s)
  [gateway] ready
  [shutdown] completed cleanly in 77ms
  ```

  **SIGINT during install triggers clean abort:**

  ```console
  $ set -m; (bash scripts/install.sh --no-onboard --no-prompt 2>&1) & PID=$!; sleep 2; kill -INT -- -$PID; wait $PID; echo "exit=$?"
  Preparing installer interface...
    OpenClaw Installer
  Detected: macos
  [1/3] Preparing environment
  Node.js found
  [2/3] Installing OpenClaw
  ! Installation interrupted
  exit=130
  ```

  **All 49 install script tests pass:**

  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts
   ✓ |tooling| install-sh.test.ts (49 tests) 4056ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  49 passed (49)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The compiled production code at `dist/onboard-helpers-D47vq5rt.js:30` calls `runtime.exit(130)` instead of `runtime.exit(0)` when the user cancels at a Clack prompt. The install script at line 2835 detects `doctor_exit == 130` and calls `abort_install_int`, which exits with 130 and the "Installation interrupted" message. Terminal output confirms: (1) doctor runs correctly on the live deployment, (2) SIGINT during install triggers clean abort with exit 130, (3) the gateway loads the patched module without errors.
- **What was not tested:** Live interactive Ctrl+C at a real Clack upgrade-doctor prompt (requires a state where doctor triggers an interactive migration question). The exit code propagation from `guardCancel(130)` through `run_doctor` to `abort_install_int` was verified via the compiled bundle, install script grep, and the 49-test suite.

target.title: fix(exec): hide policy controls from model args
target.body:
## Summary

- Remove operator-only exec controls (`security`, `ask`, `host`, `elevated`, `node`) from the model-facing exec tool schema
- Stop consuming those fields from exec tool-call args; configured tool defaults and approval defaults now remain the only policy source
- Move trusted chat command policy overrides into exec defaults instead of per-call args
- Update regression coverage for model-supplied policy fields, configured host/node routing, elevated defaults, and agent-specific exec defaults

Fixes #75811.

## Security

This closes the model-controlled exec policy boundary described in #75811. Model tool calls can still provide command/runtime inputs (`command`, `workdir`, `env`, `yieldMs`, `background`, `timeout`, `pty`), but cannot lower/raise security, approval, host routing, elevation, or node selection through tool arguments.

## Test plan

- pnpm test src/agents/bash-tools.schemas.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.exec.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.schema.test.ts

target.title: fix(slack): make tool-progress draft preview ephemeral; preserve agent text segments
target.body:
## Problem

The Slack `Working…\n• …` draft preview is meant to scaffold tool-execution visibility while an agent runs. Today, those scaffolding messages linger as separate Slack messages every time the agent emits a new assistant-message segment mid-turn, or finalizes after a tool burst. A 5-step turn ends up with ~5 `Working…` preview messages plus the final answer — visible fan-out across boundaries.

Telegram's lane stream already handles this: it edits one message and finalizes to the answer. This PR brings Slack to parity by making the draft preview fully ephemeral while preserving every agent text segment as a permanent Slack message.

## Approach

Tracks a per-draft kind: `'narrative' | 'bullets' | null`. Transitions:

| Trigger | Action |
|---|---|
| narrative → bullets | rotate (`forceNewMessage`); preserve narrative as a permanent Slack message |
| bullets → narrative | `await draftStream.clear()` (chat.delete); start a fresh draft for narrative |
| boundary, narrative | rotate (preserve) |
| boundary, bullets | clear (delete) |
| `kind=final` + bullets | clear + `deliverNormally` (answer is a fresh permanent message) |
| cleanup, bullets still open | clear |

The bullets→narrative transition AWAITs `clear()` (and so makes `updateDraftFromPartial` async) to avoid a race where the throttled draft loop could `chat.update` the bullet message with narrative text BEFORE `clear`'s synchronous step zeroed the cached `messageId` — and then `clear`'s `chat.delete` would proceed against the (now-narrative) message, destroying the narrative.

`shouldEnableSlackPreviewStreaming` also drops the carve-out that kept DM-without-thread off. Without that, the preview never appears in the most common Slack scenario (DMs with the bot, where replies don't auto-thread by default), and every mid-turn tool/block payload falls back to a fresh `chat.postMessage` instead.

## End state for a typical turn
- Each agent text segment posts as a permanent Slack message that edits in real time as tokens stream in (unchanged from before).
- Tool calls render as a single `Working…\n• …` bullet preview that edits via `chat.update` as new tools fire (unchanged from before).
- When a tool burst ends and the agent emits more text — or the turn finalizes — the bullet preview is `chat.delete`'d. The channel ends the turn with only the agent's text content.

## Tests
- Updated `dispatch.streaming.test.ts` to reflect the always-on preview-streaming behaviour for non-off modes (3 cases adjusted, no new cases needed).
- **89 files / 894 tests pass**.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` clean.

## Compatibility
- No new dependencies, no API surface changes, no config changes.
- The `streaming.preview.toolProgress` config flag still gates whether bullets are rendered at all — when off, the new ephemeral logic is dormant (no bullets to delete).
- Apps registered as Slack 'Agents & AI Apps' using `chat.startStream` are unaffected (`useStreaming` short-circuits before the draft path).

This was developed against `blink-new/openclaw` (a small downstream fork) and validated end-to-end on production Blink Claw agents on Fly.io across multiple iterations.

target.title: fix: harden subagent completion announce cleanup races
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a set of subagent completion lifecycle races that can leave completed child runs stuck behind cleanup/announce sequencing or overwrite a terminal error with a later success signal.

The patch makes three related changes:

- Do not block completion announce delivery on browser-session cleanup. The cleanup is now started after the announce/cleanup flow is leased and runs best-effort in the background, with warnings captured instead of blocking the lifecycle path.
- Skip waiting for an active embedded child session when a frozen final reply is already available. If we already captured the reply text, the requester can be notified immediately instead of waiting on a stale/active child run check.
- Preserve a terminal error outcome if a later completion signal reports success for the same run, unless the run was actually restarted after the error. This prevents cleanup/finalization races from turning a failed run into a successful task after the fact.

## Why this matters

The lifecycle path has a few asynchronous pieces that can finish in different orders:

1. child session/browser cleanup
2. completion announce delivery back to the requester
3. task finalization bookkeeping
4. embedded child-run settle checks
5. terminal lifecycle hooks

Before this patch, a slow or stuck browser cleanup could delay the announce/cleanup flow. Separately, if a child run had already produced frozen reply text but still appeared active, the announce flow could wait unnecessarily. And in a failure path, a later `complete` event could overwrite the already-recorded terminal error.

That combination makes subagent completion feel flaky from the user's side: the work is done, but the parent session may not receive the completion promptly, or the persisted final task state can become misleading.

## Implementation notes

### Lifecycle cleanup lease

`completeSubagentRun` now preclaims cleanup when `triggerCleanup` is true and cleanup has not already completed/been handled. That lease is passed into `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` as `skipBeginCleanup`, avoiding a second `beginSubagentCleanup` race while still protecting duplicate cleanup attempts.

If cleanup is not actually triggered, or announce is suppressed for steer-restart, the preclaim is released so the record does not stay permanently handled.

### Browser cleanup no longer blocks announce cleanup

Previously browser cleanup was awaited before starting the subagent announce cleanup flow. This patch starts the announce cleanup flow first, retires run-mode bundle MCP runtime as before, and then launches browser cleanup as a best-effort async task:

- uses injected `cleanupBrowserSessionsForLifecycleEnd` when provided
- lazy-loads the browser cleanup helper otherwise
- logs warning context on failure
- does not reject or delay completion delivery

### Frozen reply short-circuit

`runSubagentAnnounceFlow` now checks whether `roundOneReply` already contains non-empty frozen reply text. If it does, the flow does not wait for `waitForEmbeddedPiRunEnd` even if `isEmbeddedPiRunActive` reports true. The frozen reply is already the durable completion payload; waiting only increases the chance of a stale active-state race.

### Terminal error preservation

When a run already has a terminal error outcome/reason, a later successful completion signal no longer overwrites it unless the entry's `startedAt` proves the run restarted after the error's `endedAt`. The ended hook and persisted outcome use the preserved effective reason/outcome.

## Tests

Added/updated coverage for:

- announcing without waiting for browser cleanup
- skipping announce delivery when completion messages are disabled
- delete-mode cleanup when completion messages are disabled
- preserving terminal errors against later success completion
- allowing success to replace error only after a restart
- using frozen reply text without waiting on an active embedded child session

Verification run locally:

```bash
pnpm test src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts
```

Result: 2 files passed, 29 tests passed.
